text.title: Accessibility-First Design Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Accessibility-First Design methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Spec MUST pin heading hierarchy + landmark regions + reading order BEFORE visual treatment.
rule.rationale: SR users read structure, not pixels; structure defined late produces brittle ARIA workarounds.

rule.statement: Spec MUST include a focus-order diagram showing tab sequence + return-focus targets for any modal / drawer.
rule.rationale: Implicit focus order produces traps and skipped controls; explicit diagram makes review possible.

rule.statement: ARIA usage starts at zero. Each `aria-*` added MUST cite a rationale (native fallback wouldn't work).
rule.rationale: 'No ARIA is better than bad ARIA' — ARIA additions without rationale are net-negative.

rule.statement: Spec MUST cite contrast ratio per token used (≥4.5:1 text, ≥3:1 UI) and target size (≥24×24 CSS px for AA; ≥44×44 for AAA / mobile).
rule.rationale: Design tokens drift; pinning ratios in the spec catches drift before code.

rule.statement: Any animation MUST ship a `prefers-reduced-motion` alternate in the same spec; deferred / unspecified motion alternates are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Vestibular disorders trigger on default animations; reduced-motion is non-optional.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-accessibility-first-design.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
